source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
We are a profitable, mission-driven fintech company building financial solutions for people whose needs go unmet by traditional institutions – people with disabilities, older adults, and people in recovery, and the families and professionals who care for them. We get to wake up every day knowing our work has a profound impact on our customers' lives, and we run the business with the discipline to keep doing it for the long haul. That track record has earned us a spot on the Forbes Fintech 50 and the Inc. 5000 list of America's fastest-growing companies for the last two years.



We're hiring a Principal Product Manager to lead us in building the right thing for our bank and card partnership-based capabilities. You’ll shape what we deliver to our Family Banking partners and their account holders, balancing customer needs, partner realities, technical constraints, regulatory considerations, and business strategy. This is a senior individual contributor role for someone who works hypothesis-first, and is comfortable in the complexity of financial services.



You’ll love this role if you’re energized by difficult product problems with real-world consequences, enjoy working directly with sophisticated external partners and clients, and want to help shape the next generation of family banking. Because our products sit at the intersection of banking, payments, operations, compliance, and deeply human customer needs, the work requires rigor, judgment, and unusually strong cross-functional thinking. Our customers and partners depend on us to execute thoughtfully and reliably - and we care deeply about getting it right.



What You’ll Do

 - Own product areas related to our card and family banking partner ecosystem, helping shape both near-term execution and long-term strategy.

 - Work directly with bank partners and sophisticated external clients to understand needs, shape a compelling “why,” solve problems collaboratively, and turn partnership opportunities into scalable products and systems.

 - Build full-stack fintech products spanning customer experience, operational workflows, APIs, payment rails, and partner infrastructure.

 - Partner deeply with engineering, design, operations, compliance, customer support, and go-to-market teams to ensure we are building the right things in the right sequence - and shipping them with high quality.

 - Use AI, prototyping, data, customer conversations, and direct product exploration to accelerate learning and compress feedback loops.

 - Bring clarity to ambiguity. Navigate competing priorities, evolving constraints, and operational complexity while helping the broader organization stay focused on what matters most.

 - Lead through influence, judgment, and execution rather than org structure or management authority.

 - Go deep when execution requires it - refining edge cases, writing clear specs, improving operational processes, and unblocking teams.



Who You Are

 - An experienced product leader with 10+ years in product management and deep fintech experience, with strong instincts around how financial products actually work in the real world.

 - Someone who loves being an individual contributor and wants to stay close to the work: solving problems, shipping products, refining systems, and collaborating directly with teammates and customers.

 - Comfortable moving fluidly between big-picture strategy and nitty-gritty implementation details, especially in complex, fast-moving environments with many stakeholders and competing constraints.

 - Strong with both external partners and internal stakeholders - thoughtful, credible, low-ego, and able to build trust quickly across a wide range of audiences.

 - Technically curious and architecturally literate. You want to understand how the systems work underneath the product, not just what appears on the surface.

 - Motivated more by customer impact, execution, and building excellent products than by status, empire-building, or trend-chasing.

source_jobs.title: Senior Front End Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are a profitable, mission-driven fintech company building financial solutions for people whose needs go unmet by traditional institutions – people with disabilities, older adults, and people in recovery, and the families and professionals who care for them. We get to wake up every day knowing our work has a profound impact on our customers' lives, and we run the business with the discipline to keep doing it for the long haul. That track record has earned us a spot on the Forbes Fintech 50 and the Inc. 5000 list of America's fastest-growing companies for the last two years.



In this role, you’ll use your front end expertise to make this thoughtful fintech work happen! While you’ll spend most of your time hands-on coding in our Rails/React stack, you’ll also be taking the time to understand the architecture, mentor and learn from those around you to create more sustainable front end software



What You’ll Do

 - Shape and deliver user-centric features in our Rails/React environment

 - Act as a kind educator and learn from teammates across different experience levels in JavaScript

 - Guide the team towards creating more robust front end experiences through a mix of modeling better practices and active mentoring and teaching



Who You Are

 - Experienced JavaScript developer with modern framework experience

 - Comfortable collaborating with designers and product managers to balance tradeoffs between addressing tech debt and delivering functionality

 - Experienced teacher or mentor who also learns from others

 - Adept at delivering ambitious projects quickly while reinforcing a culture of excellence

 - Quality focused via good usability patterns and developer testing practices



What Tech You Know

 - Javascript

 - React

 - A11y

 - Material UI

 - Jest with React Testing Library
   



Why You’ll Love Us

 - We operate with the pace of a startup, the rigor of a financial services firm, and the mission-orientation of a company that deeply cares about the people it serves.

 - With fifty million Americans in our addressable market, we are building for the millions of people who need our products and services across the country - and we’ve got the potential to reach many more.

 - We have the privilege of providing financial protection for vulnerable members of society and partnering with expert service providers to do so. Our customers and partners love us, and we love them back. You can sleep soundly every night knowing you are doing a great thing by getting our services into more hands.

 - We treat one another like we treat our customers: with respect and dignity. Diversity, equity, and inclusion are cultural values we take very seriously, and we welcome and recruit team members of all backgrounds and identities.

 - We offer competitive wages, health, dental & vision coverage, paid parental leave, 12 paid holidays each year, your own professional development budget, and generous vacation.

 - The majority of us work from home from all across the US, and we enjoy connecting virtually for laughs with teammates and meeting up in-person: we have small offices in SF and in Kingston NY, and we plan team get-togethers around the country.

 

If you are a high-performing person who wants to join a diverse, inclusive team and use your skills to make a real impact in people’s lives, we invite you to apply to join our team.

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
We are a profitable, mission-driven fintech company building financial solutions for people whose needs go unmet by traditional institutions – people with disabilities, older adults, and people in recovery, and the families and professionals who care for them. We get to wake up every day knowing our work has a profound impact on our customers’ lives, and we run the business with the discipline to keep doing it for the long haul. That track record has earned us a spot on the Forbes Fintech 50 and the Inc. 5000 list of America’s fastest-growing companies for the last two years.



Design plays an important strategic role at True Link. We're looking for a Principal Product Designer to join our lean and senior product team as a senior individual contributor. Someone who can operate with a high degree of independence, lead customer insight and research initiatives, and own design quality across two of our core business lines. You’ll act as a design consultant, identifying and advocating for high impact areas per the company OKRs, and for strategic focus to bring customer insights to the table and improve the user experience, in addition to supporting in-flight product initiatives.



You'll work day-to-day with our product managers and engineering leads, reporting to the Head of Design. With you, it will be a design team of two; so high autonomy, but still someone to collaborate with. This isn't a role where you'll wait to be handed a brief - you'll be expected to identify what questions need answering, go get the answers, and translate insight into design decisions that move the product forward.



Our users are wonderfully complex and our products solve real, meaningful problems. We are proud to serve family caregivers, professional fiduciaries, trust administrators, people in recovery, and the people who love and support them.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead end-to-end product design for our Card Platform. From mobile UX iterations of our core product, to iterations of new products as we expand into adjacent categories, to experimental product led growth and AI-powered features like receipt management.

 - Support the design needs of our Trust & Invest business, including micro-iterations on the product, and solving for customer requests.

 - Drive customer insight and research. Identify the open questions that matter; proactively run customer interviews, usability tests, and concept testing; and synthesize findings into clear design implications for both near-term decisions and longer-horizon roadmap thinking.

 - Be the team's strongest advocate for the user perspective in cross-functional discussions, deepening understanding of our customers across the company.

 - Operate as a senior design thought-partner for product and engineering leadership to shape requirements, not just respond to them, which includes navigating stakeholder feedback and applying good judgment about customer discovery versus forward momentum.

 - Represent design in client-facing settings where needed.

 - Produce polished, well-considered design work using our existing design patterns - we're not looking for someone who wants to rethink the foundations. Maintain design coherence across a product suite that serves multiple user types with meaningfully different needs.

 - Handle the full range of design needs: big new features, incremental iterations, mobile polish, and the occasional slide deck or screenshot pull for the sales team.
   

Who You Are

 - An experienced product design leader with 8+ years of product design experience, especially in lean team environments. Experience in fintech, financial services, or a regulated product environment is a plus.

 - Lead with the user. Research and insight generation is something you proactively do, not something you wait to be assigned to you.

 - Stellar end-to-end design chops, from early discovery and concept development through detailed interaction design and QA.

 - You love being an individual contributo

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are a profitable, mission-driven fintech company building financial solutions for people whose needs go unmet by traditional institutions – people with disabilities, older adults, and people in recovery, and the families and professionals who care for them. We get to wake up every day knowing our work has a profound impact on our customers' lives, and we run the business with the discipline to keep doing it for the long haul. That track record has earned us a spot on the Forbes Fintech 50 and the Inc. 5000 list of America's fastest-growing companies for the last two years.



In this role, you’ll use your software development skills to make this thoughtful fintech work happen! While you’ll spend most of your time hands-on coding in our Rails/React stack, you’ll also be taking the time to understand the architecture, mentor and learn from those around you to create sustainable software.



Our engineering team carefully balances the needs of speedily pushing out new features while supporting an ever-growing portfolio of existing features. We take pride in lending our technical skills toward a social mission we care about. If this sounds like your kind of environment, please apply.



What You’ll Do

 - Independently and reliably deliver code in our Rails/React environment

 - Support and be supported by your teammates

 - Act as a kind educator and learn from teammates across different experience levels in Rails

 - Be known for your boring, no-surprises software delivery

 - Apply appropriate patterns for testing, accessibility, security, and maintainability

 - Incrementally address technical debt without losing focus on feature delivery

 - Guide the team through a mix of modeling better practices, active mentoring, and teaching



Who You Are

 - Experienced webapp developer familiar with Rails

 - Proactive problem solver who enjoys deeply understanding business needs

 - Equally comfortable working independently or closely with (remote-first) teammates

 - Experience with a modern frontend framework (React, Angular, or Vue)

 - Experience in high-compliance environments (ex: PCI, ISO 27001, HIPAA, SOX)

 - Enjoy working cross-functionally in a remote-first, agile-style environment

 - Curious about the needs of your users, teammates, and organization

 - Skilled at delivering and receiving feedback with teammates

 - Truly care about diversity and inclusion in the workplace

 - Comfortable collaborating with product managers and designers to better understand business needs

 - Comfortable existing in and seeking direction in an environment of ambiguity and change
   
   

Not Required, but Nice to Have:

 - Experience in fintech

 - Experience with fiduciary or advisory services

 - Experience with learning customer needs in B2B environments

 - Experienced teacher or mentor

 - Experience with React, ES6, yarn, webpack



Why You’ll Love Us

 - We operate with the pace of a startup, the rigor of a financial services firm, and the mission-orientation of a company that deeply cares about the people it serves.

 - With fifty million Americans in our addressable market, we are building for the millions of people who need our products and services across the country - and we’ve got the potential to reach many more.

 - We have the privilege of providing financial protection for vulnerable members of society and partnering with expert service providers to do so. Our customers and partners love us, and we love them back. You can sleep soundly every night knowing you are doing a great thing by getting our services into more hands.

 - We treat one another like we treat our customers: with respect and dignity. Diversity, equity, and inclusion are cultural values we take very seriously, and we welcome and recruit team members of all backgrounds and identities.

 - We offer competitive wages, health, dental & vision coverage, paid parental leave, 12 paid holidays each year, your own professional development budget, and generous

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, GTM & Business Operations
source_jobs.description:
We are a profitable, mission-driven fintech company building financial solutions for people whose needs go unmet by traditional institutions – people with disabilities, older adults, and people in recovery, and the families and professionals who care for them. We get to wake up every day knowing our work has a profound impact on our customers' lives, and we run the business with the discipline to keep doing it for the long haul. That track record has earned us a spot on the Forbes Fintech 50 and the Inc. 5000 list of America's fastest-growing companies for the last two years.



High performers in search of both business and social impact usually have to choose one. This role doesn't ask you to. As Senior Manager, Go-to-Market & Business Operations, you'll operate as a shared strategic leader across growth and operations for True Link’s flagship Visa Card. You'll work with card business leaders to own cross-functional initiatives end to end — structuring the approach, driving execution, and delivering the outcome.



Our ideal candidate has likely spent 5+ years building structured problem-solving, client management, and analytical skills — first in consulting or banking, then ideally at a growth-stage startup — and is ready to apply that toolkit in a place where the impact is magnified.




What You’ll Do

 - Own a rotating portfolio of high priority strategic and operational projects, spanning growth, operations, and new verticals. The scope is intentionally broad because our priority initiatives usually cut across functions.

 - Lead projects end-to-end. You'll own the problem from first principles through delivery, setting the strategy, building the plan, aligning our team, and doing the hands-on work to drive results.

 - Power the growth engine of our flagship product. Contribute to the experimentation, architecture, and launch of new verticals and product offerings — from insight gathering and entry plan through defining infrastructure and go-to-market experimentation.

 - Partner with company leadership to carry high priority initiatives to completion. When something consequential needs a cross-functional leader to take it from a priority to resolved, you'll bring structure, momentum, and a long-term view.

 - Partner with senior leaders on strategic decision-making around growth. As we weigh major decisions around new products, partners, pricing decisions, and more, you'll bring analytical rigor and structure that shapes where we land.



Who You Are

 - 4-7+ years of relevant experience (but we weigh the quality of your thinking over the exact count).

 - You have the rigor, structured problem-solving, and sharp communication often built in management consulting or a similar environment, and the agency and speed often honed in a growth-stage startup. We care deeply about this combination of skills, but not the exact path to getting there.

 - You've owned and delivered on a topline business goal end-to-end – defining the plan, assembling the people, and managing across functions to deliver results.

 - You operate independently at a high level, with the persistence, humility, and detail orientation to match.

 - You’re a structured thinker and communicator; able to bring clarity to ambiguous situations without waiting for perfect information.

 - You learn quickly and build command of a new domain, industry, or regulatory context (and ideally already have a working foundation in financial services broadly).

 - You build trust effectively with internal teams, external partners, and clients.

 - You’re fluent with data, from defining the question correctly to learning the details of our data model to owning the analysis and communication of it. Advanced Excel/Sheets required; SQL, Python, or equivalent data-extraction skills are a strong plus.



Why You’ll Love Us

 - We operate with the pace of a startup, the rigor of a financial services firm, and the mission-orientation of a company that deeply cares about the peo

notes: Fintech company with a strong focus on product and engineering roles
